name: Rhythmic Retro Racer
short_description: Rhythmic Retro Racer is a music-first rhythm game set in a mysterious 90s retro room. Hit precisely crafted rhythms built with professional tools and lose yourself in a powerful synthwave soundtrack. As the tension grows, you realize you are not playing a game, you are part of an experiment.
detailed_description:
Update Notes

v 2

Patch Notes – Major Update

New Scenario

  • The game now includes a new narrative layer, expanding the original story.

  • Environmental storytelling has been reworked to support the updated scenario.

  • Subtle narrative clues and unsettling moments have been added across the experience.

Rhythm System Upgrade

  • Rhythm sequences have been re-authored using professional beatmapping tools (Arrow Vortex).

  • Timing, note density, and structure are now more precise and consistent with the soundtrack.

  • Improved synchronization between audio, visuals, and player input.

Atmosphere & Flow

  • Improved pacing in key sequences.

  • Minor visual and audio adjustments to strengthen immersion.

Fixes & Improvements

  • Various stability and performance improvements.

  • Minor bug fixes and polish.

if you experiencing any problem please contact us

berzahgames@gmail.com


About the Game

Rhythmic Retro Racer is a music-focused rhythm game built around carefully authored timing and atmosphere. The game takes place in a mysterious room inspired by 90s retro aesthetics, where you select VHS tapes and enter structured rhythm sequences. The room is not a menu — it is part of the narrative.

At its core, the gameplay focuses on precisely authored rhythm patterns, created using professional rhythm composition tools. Notes are not randomly generated or loosely synced. Each sequence is intentionally designed to match the structure, intensity, and flow of the soundtrack.

The soundtrack is a central pillar of the experience. Powerful synthwave tracks drive both gameplay and mood, while visuals, pacing, and tension are shaped around the music rather than reacting to it.

As you progress, subtle narrative elements and unsettling moments begin to surface. Without explicit exposition, the game slowly reveals that what you are doing is not entertainment, but part of a larger, uneasy experiment. The sense of control fades, replaced by curiosity, tension, and discomfort.

Rhythmic Retro Racer blends rhythm gameplay with psychological tension, retro-futuristic atmosphere, and a mysterious narrative that unfolds through play — not dialogue.

This is a game about music, precision, and the feeling that something is not quite right.
